title: Civilian deaths from violence in 2003-2011
pages_or_sections: Overview findings; section on coalition-caused deaths and 'Shock and Awe'
strengths: Directly reports overall documented civilian deaths and the documented coalition-caused subset, and gives a clear opening-phase coalition-attributed toll.
limitations: Covers civilians only and does not provide exposure denominators or full combatant totals.
notes: Retained phrases include: violent civilian deaths exceeded 114,000 by end 2011; 15,164 directly caused by the US-led coalition; over 6,698 by April 9, nearly all attributable to coalition forces.

title: A dossier of civilian casualties 2003-2005
pages_or_sections: Executive findings on total civilian deaths and coalition share
strengths: Dedicated two-year civilian fatality dossier with actor-share attribution.
limitations: Retained accessible text is summary-level and still civilian-only; it does not solve denominator or combatant-loss gaps.
notes: Retained summary wording: 24,865 civilian deaths from March 2003 to March 2005; US and allies responsible for 37% / 9,270 deaths.

title: Iraqi civilian violent deaths by perpetrator, 2003–2008
strengths: Useful qualitative reminder that many civilian deaths were inflicted by unknown perpetrators.
limitations: Not used for episode-level numeric estimates in this retrofit because the retained text here is qualitative.
notes: Helps justify leaving many Iraq inflicting-side fields null.

title: Iraq Body Count 2003-2013: analysis of war deaths
pages_or_sections: Sections on the first-year coalition share, the March 2006-March 2008 peak, and coalition military deaths
strengths: Provides a clean two-year peak figure and a coalition military death total.
limitations: The coalition military total covers ten years and is not a full all-side combatant total.
notes: Retained summary wording includes: some 52,000 died from March 2006 to March 2008; total US and coalition military killed over ten years was 4,804.

title: Casualties of Support
pages_or_sections: Yearly civilian-death table used for 2010 and 2011
strengths: Provides separate annual civilian death totals for the drawdown years.
limitations: Still does not separate perpetrators well enough for a side-attributed episode total.
notes: Retained figures used here: 4,110 in 2010 and 4,147 in 2011.
